_comment: Special-element annotation overlay for Book I Episode 2. Two concordance entries: page 36 line 17 ('upside-down backwards E') and page 44 line 25 ('The Ballad of Persse O'Reilly' sheet music). Source: Hart Concordance Symbols and Music pages.
source: Hart Concordance Symbols page ('36.17: upside-down backwards E'); Hart Concordance Music page ('44.25: The Ballad of Persse O'Reilly'); 1939 edition screenshot confirming Ǝ at 036.17.
encoding_note: See data/annotations/README.md for the full sigla Unicode reference table.

Ǝ.note: Confirmed as Ǝ from 1939 edition screenshot. Same visual form as HCE's siglum at I.5 119.17. McHugh (1976) p.145: 'used by M to address the cad at 036.17'. Stripped with no space gap — immediately preceded '!'.

music.note: Printed sheet music (musical staff with notation for the tune of the Ballad) appearing before or within the ballad lyrics. The musical notation itself was a printed image; completely absent from the Trent corpus. Only the lyrics survive.

annotations.note: Concordance 036.17. Confirmed Ǝ (U+018E) from 1939 edition screenshot. McHugh: 'used by M to address the cad'. Stripped with no space gap — '!' immediately follows in the corpus. 1939 text: 'signlore his gesture meaning: Ǝ!) pointed at an angle of thirty'. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 036.17.
